page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.large is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 6149 processor running at a base clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the ecs.hfg5.large in the lower performance tiers. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is rated as weak, while multi-core performance falls into the bottom 10% of tested instances. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with uncached read and write speeds measuring under 10 GB/sec. This performance profile is reflected in application-level benchmarks, where static web serving throughput reaches 1.49 GB/sec and Redis SET operations achieve 1.78 million operations per second.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation, the instance avoids the CPU credit limitations of burstable types, yet its overall compute and memory throughput remain low compared to modern hardware generations. From a cost-efficiency perspective, it serves as a low-cost entry point for workloads requiring dedicated CPU cores without high resource density. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, small-scale database operations, or development and testing environments that do not require high-throughput memory or storage performance.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.large is an entry-level, x86_64 architecture virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 6149 processor clocked at 3.1 GHz. Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench results placing the instance in the lower percentiles for both single-core and multi-core compute tasks. Memory bandwidth is similarly constrained, with uncached read and write speeds under 10 GB/sec. While the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution without burstable credit limitations, the overall resource density is low. This instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, basic web serving, development environments, and small-scale database operations where high-performance throughput is not a critical requirement.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.large is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, designed for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.large is a 2-vCPU, 8.0 GB RAM virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 6149 processor for basic general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual machine with balanced memory for entry-level workloads, basic web serving, and lightweight development environments.
bullet_points: Two dedicated Intel Xeon 6149 CPU cores running at 3.1 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
